title: Merge K Sorted Lists
problem_statement: Merge k sorted linked lists into one sorted linked list and return it.

examples.explanation: All lists are merged into one sorted list.

constraints: The total number of nodes is in the range [0, 10^5]
intuition: Use a min-heap to repeatedly select the smallest node among the list heads.

approaches.name: Heap merge
approaches.description: Insert the head of each list into a min-heap, then poll the smallest and push its next node until all nodes are merged.
approaches.why_not: Pairwise merge is simpler but can be slower when k is large.

interview_qa.a: It compares node values to always select the smallest current candidate.

interview_qa.q: How many nodes are ever in the heap?
interview_qa.a: At most k nodes, one from each list.
